description: Tpm2Backend uses a single struct with a Mode enum to handle both NV storage and signing key operations. Calling get()/put() on a key-mode instance, or sign()/public_key_der()/algorithm() on an NV-mode instance, returns a runtime SecretError::Backend. This is by design (the URI determines the mode), but it means callers who construct via the inventory dispatch system get an Arc<dyn SecretStore> for a key URI that will always fail on get(). The PKCS#11 backend avoids this because it uses the same struct for all operations and detects the object class at call time. Consider: (a) having the inventory registration callbacks reject URIs that don't match the registered trait (e.g. don't register a key URI as SecretStore), or (b) documenting that callers must match URI mode to trait usage.
title: NV mode errors on sign(); key mode errors on get() — no compile-time enforcement
